mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation as measured by rising CPIAUCSL compresses real yields and increases term premium demanded by investors, directly lifting nominal 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year correlation between CPIAUCSL level and subsequent 20-day 10y yield change is positive in fewer than 50% of overlapping windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 4.5% signals labor market slack that pushes the Federal Reserve toward easier policy, reducing short-term rate expectations and pulling the 10-year yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day yield change following UNRATE >4.5% is not negative or is not significantly different from periods when UNRATE ≤4.5% (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in both CPIAUCSL (inflation) and PAYEMS (employment) indicates overheating that forces the Fed to tighten policy, raising the entire term structure including the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both CPIAUCSL and PAYEMS show positive month-over-month growth for two consecutive months
failure_condition: When both proxies meet the trigger, the subsequent 20-day yield change is negative more often than positive, or the signal's hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Within a sustained expansion (GDPC1 > 24000 and INDPRO > 100), a sharp monthly rise in HOUST greater than 5% reinforces existing demand momentum and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the regime is active and the trigger fires, the average 20-day yield change is not positive or the hit rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO greater than 1% signals an abrupt contraction in industrial activity that lowers inflation expectations and drives a flight to quality, reducing the 10-year yield within five trading days.
failure_condition: After a trigger event, the 5-day yield change is negative fewer than 60% of times or the median change is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive month-over-month growth in both RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates broad-based demand strength that raises real activity and inflation expectations, lifting the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: In quarters when both proxies grow, the 60-day yield return is negative in more than one-third of instances, or the interaction term in a regression of yield change on the two series is not statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized quarterly GDPC1 growth exceeding 3% signals robust aggregate demand that tightens resource utilization and pushes up long-term rates as the market anticipates a higher neutral rate, driving the 10-year yield higher over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The average 60-day yield change following a trigger event is not positive, or the signal is not robust in at least 70% of quarters with available data.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS greater than 2% signals a sudden fall in consumer spending that depresses growth expectations and prompts a lower rate outlook, pushing the 10-year yield down within five trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day yield change after a trigger event is negative less than 55% of the time, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below zero over all historical instances.
